MASTER_OF_MANKIND-PRIMARCH_SIGNALS.title: Signals of the Lost Sons
MASTER_OF_MANKIND-PRIMARCH_SIGNALS.pages: Genetic traces scattered long ago burn again among the stars. Malcador points to four incomplete signals on the stellar map. Which son lies at the end of each signal remains unknown.
Search
Discover one of four Primarchs at random.
Stop for now
Nothing happens.
The fleet is not yet ready. You seal the coordinates and postpone the search.

[gold]Discovered: Horus Lupercal — Cthonia[/gold] The first recovered Primarch kneels before the Emperor. ‘Father, at last you have found me. Only give the word, and I shall bring the stars to their knees beneath your name.’
Claim the reward
Gain [gold]Triumph at Ullanor[/gold] and receive [red]Doubt[/red].

[gold]Discovered: Sanguinius — Baal Secundus[/gold] The winged Primarch folds his red pinions and looks up at the Emperor. ‘Father, if I am truly your son, then I believe these wings were given so that I might protect humanity.’
Claim the reward
Gain 8 Max HP and heal 8 HP.

[gold]Discovered: Roboute Guilliman — Macragge[/gold] The Primarch who has already raised a civilization bows formally to the Emperor. ‘Father, the worlds of Macragge stand ready. I shall give order and law to your dream.’
Claim the reward
Gain 150 Gold and choose a card to Upgrade.

[gold]Discovered: Perturabo — Olympia[/gold] The Primarch finishes his calculations of stone and steel and awaits the Emperor's command. ‘Father, show me the walls that must fall. Command me what shall rise in their place.’
Claim the reward
Choose 2 cards to Upgrade and receive [red]Doubt[/red].
Horus is the first to return to your side and becomes the exemplar of conquest. Yet the brightest star casts the longest shadow.
Even upon a ruined moon, he was protecting humanity. His nobility makes the wounds of the coming crusade feel lighter.
The Five Hundred Worlds beneath him were already an empire. Rarer than conquest is the knowledge of how to preserve what was won.
No fortress can endure his calculations. Every victory left unrecognized, however, builds another wall within him.
MASTER_OF_MANKIND-LORGARS_QUESTION.title: How Is Such a Man Not a God?
MASTER_OF_MANKIND-LORGARS_QUESTION.pages: ‘If one man could seize ten thousand worlds… If one man could sire sons to defend those worlds… If one man's thoughts could guide a million ships through the void… then tell me, Father. How is such a man not a god?’
+Faith, too, may be made a tool for humanity.+
Gain 200 Gold. Upgrade a random Common and Uncommon card. Lose 6 Max HP.
Remove a card from your Deck. Lose 8 HP.
Faith is denied and truth is commanded. But can a commanded truth outlive belief?
Worship binds legions and worlds together. At the same moment, the boundary between the man-emperor and the god-emperor begins to fail.
Malcador seals the record without a word. A postponed argument does not disappear; it only grows somewhere deeper.
MASTER_OF_MANKIND-KHORNE_TEMPTATION.page: A darkness larger than armor rises through red smoke. It offers no argument—only enough strength to end the war, and an enemy upon whom to spend it.
+Rage is only a tool of my will.+
+Then let the galaxy answer in blood.+
Lose 8 Max HP. Gain the [gold]Seal of Khorne[/gold].
The red shadow recedes. Its fury remains only where it belongs: beneath your command.
Eight pulses of rage brand themselves into your soul. Every battle will begin with blood already in motion.
MASTER_OF_MANKIND-TZEENTCH_TEMPTATION.pa: One shape divides into a thousand mouths and wings. Each speaks a different future, and every future is one you have almost seen before.
+Even the unseen paths shall be mine.+
Lose 9 Max HP. Gain the [gold]Seal of Tzeentch[/gold].
The futures close one by one until only the path beneath your own feet remains.
Nine paths open at once. What was once possibility becomes the shape of your first command in every battle.
MASTER_OF_MANKIND-NURGLE_TEMPTATION.page: A vast face smiles among thorns and carrion flies. It does not promise death. It promises only the peace in which wounds and despair no longer matter.
+Pain is proof that I still live.+
+If I endure, even decay is not the end.+
Lose 7 Max HP. Gain the [gold]Seal of Nurgle[/gold].
Pain returns, sharp and honest. You welcome it as evidence that your purpose remains your own.
Seven breaths of the garden settle in your lungs. Its patience will follow you into every battle.
MASTER_OF_MANKIND-SLAANESH_TEMPTATION.pa: With each broken chain the shadow assumes a more beautiful shape. It does not say that you may become perfect. It whispers that you already are—and need only abandon the final restraint.
Remove a card from your Deck.
+Without limits, there can be no failure.+
Lose 6 Max HP. Gain the [gold]Seal of Slaanesh[/gold].
The flawless outline fractures. What remains is not perfection, but discipline sharpened by refusal.
Six restraints fall away. The limits of the hand can no longer contain the reach of your desire.
